title: Finding Unknowns
description: Equal means equal. You can find a missing amount by thinking about balance, no formulas needed.

scaffolded.title: Equal means equal
scaffolded.body: A balance scale is like a seesaw, it only stays level when both sides weigh exactly the same. Here's the cool part: when the shapes on a side are identical, they each carry an equal share of the weight. Tap through to see how that helps you find a mystery weight.

steps.text: Two identical circles together balance 8 pounds. The scale is level, so both sides weigh the same.
The circles are exactly the same, so neither one is heavier. They split the 8 pounds evenly between them.
Split 8 into 2 equal groups: 8 ÷ 2 = 4. So each circle weighs 4 pounds. That's the whole trick, share the total equally. Now you try it!

scaffolded.prompt: Three identical triangles sit on one side of a scale. The other side shows 9 pounds, and the scale is balanced. How much does one triangle weigh?
scaffolded.hint: The three triangles are identical. Share the 9 pounds equally among them.

feedback.correct: Yes! Three equal triangles split 9 evenly, so each one is 3 pounds. You've got it.
incorrect: Not quite, the triangles are identical, so split 9 into 3 equal groups.
When shapes match, each one gets the same share of the total. How big is one share of 9?

explanation.title: Share it out
explanation.body: Identical shapes always carry equal weight, so matching triangles each hold the same share of the total.

scaffolded.prompt: Three identical squares balance 6 pounds. Share the 6 one-pound chips equally across the 3 squares. How many pounds should each square get?
scaffolded.hint: Try giving each square the same number of 1-pound chips.

feedback.correct: Nice sharing! Every square got the same pile, so you've found what one square weighs.
incorrect: The squares are identical, so each one needs the same number of chips.
Keep sharing until all 6 chips are used and every square has an equal pile.

explanation.title: Split into equal shares
explanation.body: When shapes match, a fair split gives each the same pile, and that pile is what one shape weighs.

scaffolded.title: Keep it fair: change both sides the same
scaffolded.body: Sometimes a mystery weight sits next to a known weight. Here's the move to uncover it: take the same amount off **both** pans. The scale stays balanced, and the mystery weight ends up all alone, easy to read. Try the move yourself below.

interaction.resultCaption: Watch the right pan: taking 2 off turned 9 into 7. The left lost its extra 2 as well, so the scale never tipped, and now the mystery block balances 7 all on its own.

scaffolded.prompt: A mystery block plus a 1-pound weight balances 7 pounds. Change the scale to uncover the block's weight, keep both pans balanced!

followUpMc.prompt: Now the mystery block stands alone. How much is it worth?

feedback.correct: Exactly! With the extra pound gone, the mystery block balances what's left.
incorrect: Look at the right pan after both sides lost a pound, what does it read now?
The mystery block matches the weight across from it. Count the pounds on the right.

scaffolded.hint: Whatever you take off the left tray, take the same off the right tray too.

feedback.correct: The scale stays balanced, and the mystery block is uncovered!
incorrect: You changed only one side, so it tipped over. Both pans have to lose the same amount.
Take the same weight off each tray, then read what the mystery block balances.

explanation.title: Both pans stay equal
explanation.body: A scale stays balanced only when both pans change by the same amount, and that is what lets you peel the extra weight away fairly.

scaffolded.prompt: Two identical boxes plus a 2-pound weight balance with 10 pounds on the other side. How much does one box weigh?
scaffolded.hint: First take the extra 2 pounds off both sides. Then share what's left between the two boxes.

feedback.correct: Yes! Clear the extra 2, then split what's left between two equal boxes, beautifully done.
incorrect: Take the 2-pound weight off both sides first. What's left for the two boxes?
Both boxes weigh the same. After removing 2, share the rest equally between them.

explanation.title: First clear the extra, then share
explanation.body: Two moves combine here: clear the known weight from both sides to stay fair, then split what remains among the matching shapes.

mastery.prompt: Three squares balance 15 pounds. One square weighs…

feedback.correct: Correct, 15 shared into 3 equal squares is 5 each.

mastery.prompt: Two triangles plus 3 pounds balance 11 pounds. One triangle weighs…

feedback.correct: Yes! Take away 3 from both sides, then share the rest between two triangles.
incorrect: Take away 3 from both sides first, then split what's left by 2.

mastery.prompt: A mystery block plus 5 pounds balances 13 pounds. The mystery block weighs…

feedback.correct: Right, take 5 off both sides and the block stands alone.
incorrect: Remove 5 from both sides. What's left on the mystery side?
